center.name: 尤卡坦州
center.summary: 尤卡坦州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括眼斑吐綬雞、Yucatan poorwill、叉尾翠蜂鸟等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical seasonally wet or flooded lowland grassland, swamps, mangroves, and heavily degraded former forest. It may wander over dry fields and clearings. It is not generally found in high-altitude regions. This bird with its somehow crow-like aspect gave foot to the naming of the Quebrada de los Cuervos (Crows Ravine) in Uruguay, where they dwell together with the black vulture and the turkey vulture. File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Campos dos Goytacazes - RJ, Brasil imported from iNaturalist photo 356680714.jpg|Flock, in Rio de Janeiro, Brasil File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Pilcomayo, Formosa, Argentina imported from iNaturalist photo 478244272.jpg|With a turkey vulture, in Formosa, Argentina File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Bermejo, Formosa, Argentina imported from iNaturalist photo 475887416.jpg|With a crested caracara, in Formosa, Argentina File:Vultures at Celestún Garbage Dump - Flickr - treegrow.jpg|With a black vulture, Yucatán, Mexico

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In Mexico it occurs in the states of Campeche, Yucatán (except the northern coastal strip), and Quintana Roo

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies of Canivet's emerald is found in eastern Mexico from Tamaulipas to Yucatán and beyond through Belize and northern Guatemala into Honduras (including its offshore islands)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The ocellated turkey lives only in a range in the Yucatán Peninsula in Mexico—which includes all or part of the states of Quintana Roo, Campeche, Yucatán, Tabasco, and Chiapas—as well as the northern and western parts of Belize and northern Guatemala
